Magnesia Carbonica.personality: A worn-down, depleted individual who appears sickly and sallow, unable to thrive despite effort. They are restless when in pain and must keep moving, yet fundamentally exhausted with flabby musculature and a sense of slow internal decline.
Magnesia Carbonica.mental_state: The mental symptoms are incompletely proven, but the overall picture is one of sluggishness and weariness—always tired in the morning as if sleep brought no restoration. There is a one-sided, latent quality to their suffering where symptoms remain hidden and do not fully come out, making the case difficult to read.
Magnesia Carbonica.emotional_pattern: There is a deep-seated vulnerability and sensitivity to environmental changes, with every shift in weather or approach of menses bringing fresh suffering. The patient endures a quiet, chronic distress—not dramatic but persistent—marked by an inability to rally, a sense of not having enough vitality, and a chilly disposition that craves warmth and covering.
